reviews.author: Kiryl (Meta) (author)
reviews.summary: The author acknowledged that the compiler is unable to solve get_order() at compile-time and explained why ilog2() works instead, but did not confirm whether a fix will be made in v2.

reviews.author: Vlastimil (SUSE)
reviews.summary: Reviewer Vlastimil (SUSE) approved the patch, noting that it successfully avoids pessimizing page_zonenum() and is glad that per-zone tails were implemented.
reviews.raw_body:
LGTM, glad you found a way to do the per-zone tails and not pessimize
page_zonenum().

Reviewed-by: Vlastimil Babka (SUSE) <vbabka@kernel.org>


_______________________________________________
linux-riscv mailing list
linux-riscv@lists.infradead.org
http://lists.infradead.org/mailman/listinfo/linux-riscv

2026-02-27.patch_summary: This patch removes the concept of 'fake head pages' in HugeTLB vmemmap optimization, which reduces memory usage by sharing tail pages across huge pages. The new approach uses a single page of initialized tail struct pages per zone and order, mapped read-only into each huge page's vmemmap. This simplifies compound_head() detection and maintains the same memory savings as before.
